studentQuestionArray.content: The nurse documents the urine output and age of some neonates as given below: Which neonate does the nurse suspect to have side effects of magnesium sulfate that is administered to the mother during labor?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales


Option 1:The normal range of urine output for the first few days of life in a full-term neonate is 15-60 mL/kg. Therefore, a urine output of 10 mL/kg in a 3-day-old neonate indicates a decrease in urinary output, which might be due to magnesium sulfate therapy in the mother during labor. Magnesium sulfate causes neuromuscular blockade resulting in urinary retention.

Option 2:A urine output of 60 mL/day in a 15-day-old neonate is a normal finding.

Option 3:Urine output increases to 250-400 mL/day by the end of first month of life. Therefore, a urine output of 265 mL/day in a 29-day-old neonate is a normal finding.

Option 4:A 30-day-old neonate who has a urine output of 300 mL/day is considered to have normal finding.

optionList.content: Feeding the baby with cow’s milk
Cuddling the infant close to the body
Placing the infant on the back to sleep
Placing the infant in the rear-facing position in car seats
Feeding the baby with cow’s milk
Cuddling the infant close to the body
Placing the infant on the back to sleep
Placing the infant in the rear-facing position in car seats

studentQuestionArray.content: A nurse is teaching health promotion interventions to a mother of a 10-month-old infant. Which activity of the mother indicates the need for further teaching?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Using cow’s milk is not recommended in infants under 1. This is because infants cannot digest the fat in cow’s milk. Cow’s milk contains low iron, which is needed for development.
Option 2:Cuddling the infant close to the body may provide warmth, motion, and security, which may help relieve colic.
Option 3:Placing infants on their backs to sleep is recommended because it helps prevent sudden infant death syndrome (SIDS).
Option 4:Rear-facing car seats are used for infants until the age of 2 to prevent injury in motor vehicle crashes.

Test Taking Tips: Knowing the nutritional requirement of an infant will help you find the answer.

studentQuestionArray.content: Which is an example of relaxation therapy?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Massage, music, holding, and positioning are all practices that are part of relaxation therapy.
Option 2:Puzzles and coloring are a kind of play therapy.
Option 3:Pain medication is part of pain relief therapy.
Option 4:Hypnosis is a psychotherapy, and may or may not put the client at ease.

optionList.content: The client uses his or her fingers to get food from serving trays in restaurants.
The client washes his or her hands on returning home from outside.
The client uses antibacterial hand gel as needed when in public places.
The client asks for a clean spoon when theirs is dropped on the floor accidentally in a restaurant.
The client uses his or her fingers to get food from serving trays in restaurants.
The client washes his or her hands on returning home from outside.
The client uses antibacterial hand gel as needed when in public places.
The client asks for a clean spoon when theirs is dropped on the floor accidentally in a restaurant.

studentQuestionArray.content: The nurse is teaching a client the measures of preventing infections. Which action of the client indicates the need for further teaching?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:The nurse should suggest that the client use tongs instead of fingers to get food from serving trays to prevent the contamination of food.
Option 2:Washing hands after returning helps to remove bacteria present of the surface of hands.
Option 3:When in public places, the client may come in contact with several surfaces and things that contain bacteria. Therefore, it is very important to use antibacterial hand gel to prevent the infections.
Option 4:When a spoon is dropped on floor in restaurant, it gets into contact with surfaces containing many infection-causing bacteria. Therefore, the nurse should suggest that the client ask for a clean spoon when it is dropped on the floor to prevent the infections.

Test Taking Tips: Basic knowledge on preventing the spread of infections in community will help you to choose the right answer.

optionList.content: Encouraging the use of maternity girdle
Teaching proper techniques for sitting up from a lying position
Encouraging the use of maternity girdle
Teaching proper techniques for sitting up from a lying position

studentQuestionArray.content: Which nursing intervention will be beneficial to a client with diastasis recti?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Pelvic tilt exercises are helpful when the client is having low back pain or joint discomfort or difficulty in walking.
Option 2:Relaxation techniques help to relieve stress and pain. Therefore, they are encouraged in clients having back pain or joint pain.
Option 3:Maternity girdle will provide support to the abdomen and enhance the circulation by reducing the venous pressure in pelvis or extremities in clients with edema.
Option 4:Separation of two rectus abdominis muscle bands at midline is called diastasis recti. Using proper body mechanics when getting up and down from a lying position helps to avoid stretching of abdominal muscles and pressure on diastasis recti.

optionList.content: Sleeping in a semi sitting position
Sleeping in a semi sitting position

studentQuestionArray.content: Which action helps to reduce obstructive sleep apnea in obese clients?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Relaxation techniques help to promote sleep in clients with insomnia and also help to relieve back pain in those clients.
Option 2:Obesity is the main reason for obstructive sleep apnea. Effects of obesity on respiratory system are decreased in a semi-sitting position. Therefore, it is reduces the obstructive sleep apnea in obese clients.
Option 3:Avoiding prolonged standing and sitting is helpful in clients with edema in lower extremities.
Option 4:Low-key activities at pre-bedtime helps to promote sleep in clients with insomnia.

optionList.content: Instructing the client to sleep in a semi-sitting position
Instructing the client to lie back with legs propped against the wall
Instructing the client to use a maternity girdle
Instructing the client to rise slowly from a lying position
Instructing the client to sleep in a semi-sitting position
Instructing the client to lie back with legs propped against the wall
Instructing the client to use a maternity girdle
Instructing the client to rise slowly from a lying position

studentQuestionArray.content: While caring for a pregnant client, the nurse finds that the client has difficulty breathing while sleeping. Which nursing interventions will be beneficial to this client?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Sleeping in a semi-sitting position enhances breathing in the clients with dyspnea.
Option 2:Lying back with legs propped against the wall will be helpful in clients with varicosities.
Option 3:A maternity girdle is an abdominal support, which helps to enhance the circulation by reducing the venous pressure in pelvis or lower extremities. Therefore, it is beneficial in clients with edema.
Option 4:Rising slowly from a lying position will help to prevent orthostatic hypotension, which is caused due to sudden posture changes.

Test Taking Tips: Recollect the concepts of Antepartum care and apply that knowledge to choose the right option.

optionList.content: The client is having nausea and vomiting.
The client is having nausea and vomiting.

studentQuestionArray.content: The nurse encourages a pregnant client to do Kegel exercises. What would be the rationale for the nurse’s action?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Maintaining regular bowel habits, chewing foods slowly and avoiding gas-producing foods help to reduce flatulence.
Option 2:The client having nasal/sinus congestion should be instructed to try a cool-air humidifier.
Option 3:Kegel exercises help to maintain pelvic floor muscle strength and decrease the risk of urinary urgency.
Option 4:The client will be instructed to avoid strong odors and causative factors such as spicy or greasy foods to prevent or reduce nausea and vomiting.

studentQuestionArray.content: The weight of a client is 45 kg and height is 1.52 meters. What does the nurse anticipate from the client’s height and weight?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:If the client’s BMI is 30 to 39, then it indicates that the client is obese.
Option 2:The body mass index (BMI) of a client can be calculated using the formula:
BMI=weight in kilograms divided by (height in meters)2
The client’s BMI is 45/ (1.52)2 = 45/2.31=19.4.
A BMI of 19 to 24 is normal.
Option 3:If the client’s BMI is less than 18, then it indicates that the client is underweight.
Option 4:If the client’s BMI is 25 to 29, then it indicates that the client is overweight.

studentQuestionArray.content: Which condition is prevented by the varicella vaccination?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:The mumps, measles, and rubella (MMR) vaccine is used for prevention of mumps.
Option 2:Measles is prevented by immunization with the mumps, measles, and rubella (MMR) vaccine.
Option 3:The diphtheria, tetanus, and pertussis (DTaP) vaccine is used for the prevention of diphtheria.
Option 4:The varicella zoster virus causes chicken pox; immunization with the varicella vaccine helps prevent it.

optionList.content: The client washes his or her hands with water only.
The client washes clothing with hot water.
The client keeps sores covered with bandages.
The client washes his or her hands with water only.
The client washes clothing with hot water.
The client keeps sores covered with bandages.

studentQuestionArray.content: The nurse is teaching a client who has a methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus infection measures to prevent the spread of infection. Which actions of the client indicate the need for additional teaching?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Showering daily with antibacterial soap will remove the microorganisms from the skin surface and help to prevent the spread of infections.
Option 2:Washing hands with water will not remove microorganisms. Hands should be washed with antibacterial soap or alcohol-based hand sanitizer.
Option 3:Washing clothes with hot water helps to kill bacteria present on the clothes and thereby prevents the spread of infection to others.
Option 4:Keeping sores covered with bandages helps to avoid frequent touching of the sores with bare hands and prevents the spread of infection.

optionList.content: “I should include more whole grains and nuts in my child’s diet.”
“I should give only 18 to 24 ounces of cow’s milk to my child every day.”
“I should give iron supplements along with orange juice to my child.”
“I should give iron supplements along with tea to my child.”
“I should include more whole grains and nuts in my child’s diet.”
“I should give only 18 to 24 ounces of cow’s milk to my child every day.”
“I should give iron supplements along with orange juice to my child.”
“I should give iron supplements along with tea to my child.”

studentQuestionArray.content: The nurse is educating the parent of a child who has iron deficiency anemia. Which statement made by the parent indicates the need for further teaching?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Whole grains, nuts, red meat, beans, and fortified cereals are good sources of iron. Hence, children with iron deficiency anemia should be given more of these foods.
Option 2:Limiting cow’s milk to 18 to 24 ounces per day to children will improve iron absorption in children.
Option 3:Iron absorption is enhanced in an acidic environment. Hence, taking iron supplements with orange or lemon juice is beneficial.
Option 4:Iron supplements should not be taken along with tea because it may adversely affect the iron absorption process.

studentQuestionArray.content: A client shows signs of premature labor during the 30th week of gestation. Which action of the primary health care provider will help to accelerate fetal lung maturation?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Indomethacin administration will help to delay the delivery for more than 48 hours.
Option 2:Betamethasone enhances the production of surfactants and promotes fetal lung maturity in preterm infants. Therefore, administering betamethasone to the client who has signs of premature labor will help to prevent respiratory distress syndrome in the infants.
Option 3:Magnesium sulfate reduces the microcapillary brain hemorrhage in preterm neonates.
Option 4:Progesterone supplements help to prevent premature birth.

studentQuestionArray.content: The hemoglobin levels of pregnant clients who are in the first trimester are given here: Which client should receive ferrous sulfate?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales


Option 1:Hemoglobin below 11 mg/dL in the first and third trimester clients indicates that they have anemia and ferrous sulfate should be given. Client 1 is having hemoglobin of 10 mg/dL. Therefore, client 1 should receive ferrous sulfate, which is iron supplementation to prevent the complications of anemia.

Option 2:Client 2 is having hemoglobin of 11mg/dL. A level below 11mg/dL indicates anemia and the client will need ferrous sulfate therapy in such situations.

Option 3:Client 3 has hemoglobin levels of 11.3 mg/dL, and so does not require ferrous sulfate therapy.

Option 4:Client 4 has hemoglobin of 11.6 mg/dL, which is indicative that the client is not anemic and does not require ferrous sulfate administration.

studentQuestionArray.content: Which cancer can be screened by a Papanicolaou (Pap) smear test?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Testicular self-exam (TSE) is an option for testicular cancer screening.
Option 2:ABCD criteria, which include asymmetry, border irregularity, and color variability, are used for assessing skin malignancy.
Option 3:Breast self-examination and mammography screens for breast cancer.
Option 4:A Papanicolaou test, or a Pap smear, is used to detect cellular changes in the cervix, which is indicated for cervical cancer screening.

Test Taking Tips: A Pap smear test is a screening method that helps diagnose cervical cancer.

studentQuestionArray.content: While reviewing a client’s laboratory reports, the nurse finds that the client has poor cardiac function. Which dietary intake should the nurse suggest to the client for promoting health? Select all that apply.
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Sardines are rich sources of phosphorous, which are useful in conditions like bone loss, osteoporosis, and poor growth.
Option 2:Dietary intake of lima beans reduces the risk of heart diseases because lima beans contain magnesium, which improves endothelial function and helps to maintain optimal cardiac rhythm.
Option 3:Wheat bran is rich in phosphorous. As phosphorous is important for bone and tooth strength, its dietary intake may help to reduce problems like poor growth and bone loss.
Option 4:Whole grains are rich in both magnesium and calcium. Calcium and magnesium play a role in conduction of heart muscles and improves cardiac functioning.
Option 5:Green leafy vegetables contain magnesium. Magnesium helps to promote optimal cardiac rhythm. It also decreases oxidative stress, inflammation, and diminishes endothelial dysfunction. Therefore, intake of green leafy vegetables will be beneficial for the client.

Test Taking Tips: Having knowledge on the minerals that play a key role in heart disease prevention and treatment will help you to choose the right answer.

optionList.content: Placing a stocking cap on the infant’s head
Placing the neonate skin to skin with the mother
Encouraging rooting reflex in the neonate
Placing a stocking cap on the infant’s head
Placing the neonate skin to skin with the mother
Encouraging rooting reflex in the neonate

studentQuestionArray.content: Which nursing interventions help to prevent the neonate’s body temperature from falling below 36°C? Select all that apply.
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Removal of wet linens helps to prevent heat loss through conduction.
Option 2:The neonate should not be given a bath until his or her core body temperature has stabilized.
Option 3:Heat loss takes place easily from a large surface area through radiation and conduction. Therefore, it is important to place a stocking cap on the neonate’s head.
Option 4:Contact of the neonate with the skin of mother provides warmth, and therefore prevents heat loss.
Option 5:Rooting reflex helps the neonate to grab the nipples quickly and ensures adequate nutrition, but does not affect his or her body temperature.

Test Taking Tips: You should recollect the measures to maintain normal body temperature to answer this question.

studentQuestionArray.content: Which components of behavior-specific cognitions and affect influence health promotion? Select all that apply.
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Activity-related affect falls under behavior-specific cognitions and affect.
Option 2:Prior related behavior falls under the individual characteristics and experiences, but not behavior-specific cognitions and affect.
Option 3:Perceived self-efficacy falls under behavior-specific cognitions and affect.
Option 4:Perceived benefits of action fall under behavior-specific cognitions and affect.
Option 5:Health-promoting behavior falls under behavioral outcome.

studentQuestionArray.content: The nurse is preparing a diet plan for a client with pernicious anemia. Which foods included in the diet plan will be beneficial to the client? Select all that apply.
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Nuts are a rich source of thiamine and pyridoxine. These vitamins are beneficial in clients with peripheral neuritis.
Option 2:Potatoes contain vitamin C, which is useful to prevent scurvy, gingivitis, and iron deficiency anemia.
Option 3:Pernicious anemia is caused due to the deficiency of vitamin B12. Dairy products such as milk and yogurt are rich in vitamin B12 (cyanacobalamine). Therefore, the intake of these products will help the clients with pernicious anemia.
Option 4:Green leafy vegetables are rich in folic acid. Folic acid deficiency can cause megaloblastic anemia.
Option 5:Animal products such as meat are a good source of vitamin B12 and reduce the risk of pernicious anemia.

Test Taking Tips: Determine which vitamin deficiency causes pernicious anemia and recall the dietary sources for that vitamin.

optionList.content: “I will limit my intake of salt.”
“I will exclude milk from my diet.”
“I will exclude yogurt from my diet.”
“I will limit my intake of salt.”
“I will exclude milk from my diet.”
“I will exclude yogurt from my diet.”

studentQuestionArray.content: A nurse is teaching a client about the methods to prevent hypertension. Which statements made by the client indicate effective teaching? Select all that apply.
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales

Option 1:Smoking causes narrowing of the arteries, which, in turn, causes hypertension. Quitting smoking will help to maintain a normal blood pressure.
Option 2:A high intake of salt, which has sodium, will elevate blood pressure levels. Therefore, limiting the intake of salt will help prevent hypertension.
Option 3:Milk is a rich source of calcium. A diet high in calcium is recommended for the prevention of hypertension.
Option 4:Exercises, mainly aerobic in nature, will reduce the risk of hypertension. Exercising daily for 30 to 45 minutes will reduce the risk of hypertension.
Option 5:Yogurt is a rich source of potassium. The client should consume a diet that is rich in potassium.
